import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
df = pd.read_csv("fifa_wc_mens_match_dataset_1970_2022.csv")
df.head()

In [126]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 1
# Which teams have the highest FIFA World Cup Win Percentage?
# ==========================================================

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Calculate total matches played by each team
matches_played = df.groupby('team_name').size()

# Calculate total matches won by each team
matches_won = df[df['result'] == 'win'].groupby('team_name').size()

# Create DataFrame
win_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Matches Played': matches_played,
    'Matches Won': matches_won
})

# Replace missing values with 0
win_df = win_df.fillna(0)

# Convert Matches Won to integer
win_df['Matches Won'] = win_df['Matches Won'].astype(int)

# Calculate Win Percentage
win_df['Win Percentage'] = (
    win_df['Matches Won'] / win_df['Matches Played']
) * 100

# Select teams that have played at least 10 matches
win_df = win_df[win_df['Matches Played'] >= 10]

# Sort by Win Percentage
top10 = win_df.sort_values(
    by='Win Percentage',
    ascending=False
).head(10)

# Display Top 10 Teams
print(top10)

# Create Plotly Horizontal Bar Chart
fig = px.bar(
    top10,
    x='Win Percentage',
    y=top10.index,
    orientation='h',
    color='Win Percentage',
    text='Win Percentage',
    color_continuous_scale='Viridis',
    title='Top 10 FIFA World Cup Teams by Win Percentage'
)

# Format Labels
fig.update_traces(
    texttemplate='%{text:.1f}%',
    textposition='outside'
)

# Improve Layout
fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text': 'Top 10 FIFA World Cup Teams by Win Percentage',
        'x': 0.5,
        'xanchor': 'center'
    },
    xaxis_title='Win Percentage (%)',
    yaxis_title='Team',
    font=dict(
        family='Arial',
        size=14
    ),
    height=650,
    width=1000,
    coloraxis_colorbar_title='Win %'
)

              Matches Played  Matches Won  Win Percentage
team_name                                                
Brazil                    62           45       72.580645
Germany                   44           30       68.181818
Argentina                 65           40       61.538462
West Germany              24           14       58.333333
France                    49           28       57.142857
Croatia                   30           17       56.666667
Netherlands               45           25       55.555556
Romania                   13            7       53.846154
Belgium                   37           19       51.351351
Spain                     45           23       51.111111
In [127]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 2
# How has the average number of goals scored per match
# changed across FIFA World Cup tournaments?
# ==========================================================

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# Calculate total goals in each match
df['Total Goals'] = df['goals_for'] + df['goals_against']

# Calculate average goals for each tournament
goals_trend = (
    df.groupby('tournament_name')['Total Goals']
      .mean()
      .reset_index()
)

# Display the data
print(goals_trend)

# Create Line Chart
fig = px.line(
    goals_trend,
    x='tournament_name',
    y='Total Goals',
    markers=True,
    title='Average Goals per Match Across FIFA World Cup Tournaments'
)

# Improve chart
fig.update_traces(
    line=dict(width=4),
    marker=dict(size=9)
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text':'Average Goals per Match Across FIFA World Cup Tournaments',
        'x':0.5
    },
    xaxis_title='Tournament',
    yaxis_title='Average Goals per Match',
    height=600,
    width=1000
)

fig.show()

              tournament_name  Total Goals
0   1970 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.733333
1   1974 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.705882
2   1978 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.812500
3   1982 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.560606
4   1986 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.532468
5   1990 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.289157
6   1994 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.704082
7   1998 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.658120
8   2002 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.515625
9   2006 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.296875
10  2010 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.265625
11  2014 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.671875
12  2018 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.640625
13  2022 FIFA Men's World Cup     2.687500
In [128]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 3
# Does higher ball possession increase the chances of winning
# a FIFA World Cup match?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Remove rows with missing possession values
possession_df = df.dropna(subset=['possession'])

# Create Scatter Plot
fig = px.scatter(
    possession_df,
    x='possession',
    y='goals_for',
    color='result',
    hover_data=['team_name', 'opponent_name', 'tournament_name'],
    title='Relationship Between Ball Possession and Match Outcome',
    labels={
        'possession': 'Ball Possession (%)',
        'goals_for': 'Goals Scored',
        'result': 'Match Result'
    },
    color_discrete_map={
        'win': 'green',
        'draw': 'orange',
        'lose': 'red'
    }
)

# Improve Layout
fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text': 'Relationship Between Ball Possession and Match Outcome',
        'x': 0.5
    },
    xaxis_title='Ball Possession (%)',
    yaxis_title='Goals Scored',
    height=650,
    width=1000
)

fig.show()
In [129]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 4
# Which FIFA World Cup stages have the highest average goals?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Create Total Goals column
df['Total Goals'] = df['goals_for'] + df['goals_against']

# Create Box Plot
fig = px.box(
    df,
    x='stage_name',
    y='Total Goals',
    color='stage_name',
    title='Goals Distribution Across FIFA World Cup Stages'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text':'Goals Distribution Across FIFA World Cup Stages',
        'x':0.5
    },
    xaxis_title='Tournament Stage',
    yaxis_title='Total Goals per Match',
    height=650,
    width=1000,
    showlegend=False
)

fig.show()
In [130]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 5
# Which teams create the highest average number of
# shots on target?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Remove missing values
shots_df = df.dropna(subset=['shots_on_target'])

# Calculate average shots on target
team_shots = (
    shots_df.groupby('team_name')['shots_on_target']
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

# Select Top 10 teams
top10_shots = (
    team_shots.sort_values(
        by='shots_on_target',
        ascending=False
    )
    .head(10)
)

# Display Table
print(top10_shots)

# Create Plotly Bar Chart
fig = px.bar(
    top10_shots,
    x='shots_on_target',
    y='team_name',
    orientation='h',
    color='shots_on_target',
    text='shots_on_target',
    color_continuous_scale='Plasma',
    title='Top 10 FIFA World Cup Teams by Average Shots on Target'
)

fig.update_traces(
    texttemplate='%{text:.2f}',
    textposition='outside'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text':'Top 10 FIFA World Cup Teams by Average Shots on Target',
        'x':0.5
    },
    xaxis_title='Average Shots on Target',
    yaxis_title='Team',
    height=650,
    width=1000,
    coloraxis_colorbar_title='Shots'
)

fig.show()

                 team_name  shots_on_target

# ==========================================================
# Question 6
# Which FIFA World Cup teams receive the highest average
# number of yellow cards?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Remove missing values
cards_df = df.dropna(subset=['yellow_cards'])

# Calculate average yellow cards
team_cards = (
    cards_df.groupby('team_name')['yellow_cards']
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

# Top 10 teams
top10_cards = (
    team_cards.sort_values(
        by='yellow_cards',
        ascending=False
    )
    .head(10)
)

print(top10_cards)

# Plot
fig = px.bar(
    top10_cards,
    x='yellow_cards',
    y='team_name',
    orientation='h',
    text='yellow_cards',
    color='yellow_cards',
    color_continuous_scale='YlOrRd',
    title='Top 10 Teams by Average Yellow Cards'
)

fig.update_traces(
    texttemplate='%{text:.2f}',
    textposition='outside'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title={
        'text':'Top 10 Teams by Average Yellow Cards',
        'x':0.5
    },
    xaxis_title='Average Yellow Cards',
    yaxis_title='Team',
    height=650,
    width=1000,
    coloraxis_colorbar_title='Yellow Cards'
)

fig.show()

                team_name  yellow_cards

In [132]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 7
# Which stadiums host the highest-scoring matches?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Total goals
df['Total Goals'] = df['goals_for'] + df['goals_against']

# Average goals by stadium
stadium_goals = (
    df.groupby('stadium_name')['Total Goals']
      .mean()
      .reset_index()
)

# Stadiums with at least 5 matches
stadium_matches = df.groupby('stadium_name').size().reset_index(name='Matches')

stadium_goals = stadium_goals.merge(stadium_matches, on='stadium_name')

stadium_goals = stadium_goals[stadium_goals['Matches'] >= 5]

top10_stadiums = stadium_goals.sort_values(
    by='Total Goals',
    ascending=False
).head(10)

print(top10_stadiums)

fig = px.bar(
    top10_stadiums,
    x='Total Goals',
    y='stadium_name',
    orientation='h',
    color='Total Goals',
    text='Total Goals',
    color_continuous_scale='Turbo',
    title='Top 10 Highest Scoring FIFA World Cup Stadiums'
)

fig.update_traces(
    texttemplate='%{text:.2f}',
    textposition='outside'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title_x=0.5,
    xaxis_title='Average Goals per Match',
    yaxis_title='Stadium',
    height=650,
    width=1000
)

fig.show()

                      stadium_name  Total Goals  Matches
132        Suwon World Cup Stadium     5.000000        8
93                     Parkstadion     4.500000        8
53               Estádio Beira-Rio     4.400000       10

24                Estadi de Sarrià     3.800000        5
27        Estadio Chateau Carreras     3.777778        9
In [133]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 8
# Relationship between corners and goals scored
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

corner_df = df.dropna(subset=['corners'])

fig = px.scatter(
    corner_df,
    x='corners',
    y='goals_for',
    color='result',
    hover_data=['team_name', 'opponent_name'],
    title='Relationship Between Corners Won and Goals Scored',
    color_discrete_map={
        'win':'green',
        'draw':'orange',
        'lose':'red'
    }
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title_x=0.5,
    xaxis_title='Corners',
    yaxis_title='Goals Scored',
    height=650,
    width=1000
)

fig.show()
In [134]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 9
# Which teams have the highest average ball possession?
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Remove missing values
possession_df = df.dropna(subset=['possession'])

# Average possession by team
team_possession = (
    possession_df.groupby('team_name')['possession']
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

# Teams with at least 10 matches
matches = df.groupby('team_name').size().reset_index(name='Matches')
team_possession = team_possession.merge(matches, on='team_name')
team_possession = team_possession[team_possession['Matches'] >= 10]

# Top 10 teams
top10_possession = team_possession.sort_values(
    by='possession',
    ascending=False
).head(10)

print(top10_possession)

fig = px.bar(
    top10_possession,
    x='possession',
    y='team_name',
    orientation='h',
    color='possession',
    text='possession',
    color_continuous_scale='Tealgrn',
    title='Top 10 FIFA World Cup Teams by Average Ball Possession'
)

fig.update_traces(
    texttemplate='%{text:.1f}%',
    textposition='outside'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title_x=0.5,
    xaxis_title='Average Ball Possession (%)',
    yaxis_title='Team',
    height=650,
    width=1000
)

fig.show()

      team_name  possession  Matches
51        Spain   64.444444       45
2     Argentina   58.322581       65
20      Germany   55.529412       44
29       Mexico   55.086957       37
9         Chile   54.625000       17
18      England   54.241379       47
6        Brazil   54.147059       62
39     Portugal   53.346154       28
31  Netherlands   52.869565       45
4       Belgium   52.789474       37
In [135]:
# ==========================================================
# Question 10
# Correlation Heatmap of Match Statistics
# ==========================================================

import plotly.express as px

# Select numerical columns
corr_columns = [
    'goals_for',
    'goals_against',
    'possession',
    'shots',
    'shots_on_target',
    'passes_completed',
    'passes_attempted',
    'corners',
    'fouls',
    'yellow_cards'
]

corr = df[corr_columns].corr()

fig = px.imshow(
    corr,
    text_auto=True,
    color_continuous_scale='RdBu_r',
    title='Correlation Between FIFA World Cup Match Statistics'
)

fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    title_x=0.5,
    height=750,
    width=900
)

fig.show()
